text_original: Motse said : All tEe riders in tEe world desire tEeir states to be wealtEy, tEeir people to be inany> and tEeir government and jurisdiction to be orderly.

text_original: But tEey do not understand to govern tEeir states and people by exaltation of tEe virtuous.

text_original: TEey Eave missed, indeed, tEe foundation of government.

text_original: But can we not point tEis out to tEem by means of parallels ?

text_original: Now, supposing, in governing Eis state, a feudal lord sEould proclaim : 'All tEose wEo can sEoot (witE an arrow) and drive (a cEariot) well I sEall reward and Eonour ; all tEose wEo can not I sEall punisE and disgrace.’’ If, tEen, we sEouid inquire among tEe people of tEe state as to wEo would rejoice and wEo would be afraid, I suppose naturally tEose wEo could sEoot and drive would rejoice and tEose wEo could not would be afraid.
text_normalized: Now, supposing, in governing Eis state, a feudal lord sEould proclaim : 'All tEose wEo can sEoot (witE an arrow) and drive (a cEariot) well I sEall reward and Eonour ; all tEose wEo can not I sEall punisE and disgrace.’’ If, tEen, we sEouid inquire among tEe people of tEe state as to wEo would rejoice and wEo would be afraid, I suppose naturally tEose wEo could sEoot and drive would rejoice and tEose wEo could not would be afraid.

text_original: I Eave followed tEis (line of argument) and led tEem supposedly to proclaim : '' All tEe loyal and faitEful ; I sEall reward and Eonour ; all tEe disloyal and unfaitEful I sEall punisE and disgrace.” If now we sEould inquire among tEe people of tEe state as to wEo would rejoice and wEo would be afraid, I suppose naturally tEe loyal and faitEful would rejoice and tEe disloyal and unfaitEful would be afraid.

text_original: Therefore tEe state and tEe people are to be governed by exalting tEe virtuous, so that those in the state that do good i will be encouraged and those that do evil will be obstructed.
text_normalized: Therefore tEe state and tEe people are to be governed by exalting tEe virtuous, so that those in the state that do good i will be encouraged and those that do evil will be obstructed.

text_original: To govern the empire seems then to consist of encouraging the good and obstructing the evil But why is it that I Eave esteemed the way of Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu ?
text_normalized: To govern the empire seems then to consist of encouraging the good and obstructing the evil But why is it that I Eave esteemed the way of Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu ?

text_original: those in the empire that did good would be encouraged and those that did evil would be obstructed.
text_normalized: those in the empire that did good would be encouraged and those that did evil would be obstructed.

text_original: So, then, the principle of exaltation of the virtuous is identical with the way of Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.
text_normalized: So, then, the principle of exaltation of the virtuous is identical with the way of Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.

text_original: The gentlemen  of to-day all exalt the virtuous in their private speech and conduct.
text_normalized: The gentlemen  of to-day all exalt the virtuous in their private speech and conduct.

text_original: But when it comes to the administration of the government for the public, they fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.
text_normalized: But when it comes to the administration of the government for the public, they fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.

text_original: Then I know the gentlemen understand only trifles and not things of significance.
text_normalized: Then I know the gentlemen understand only trifles and not things of significance.

text_original: How do I know it is so ?
text_normalized: How do I know it is so ?

text_original: Suppose the ruler had a cow or a sheep which he could not have killed, he would surely look for a skilful butcher.
text_normalized: Suppose the ruler had a cow or a sheep which he could not have killed, he would surely look for a skilful butcher.

text_original: Or if he wanted a garment which he could not have made, he would surely look for a skilful tailor.
text_normalized: Or if he wanted a garment which he could not have made, he would surely look for a skilful tailor.

text_original: For these, the ruler would not employ his relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking, because he knew clearly they were incapable.
text_normalized: For these, the ruler would not employ his relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking, because he knew clearly they were incapable.

text_original: He was afraid they would spoil the things to be attended to.
text_normalized: He was afraid they would spoil the things to be attended to.

text_original: So, in these, the rulers do not fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.
text_normalized: So, in these, the rulers do not fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.

text_original: Again, if the ruler had a sick horse that he could not have cured, he would surely look for an ex-perienced veterinary doctor.
text_normalized: Again, if the ruler had a sick horse that he could not have cured, he would surely look for an ex-perienced veterinary doctor.

text_original: Or if he had a tight bow which he could not draw, he would surely look for a skilful workman.
text_normalized: Or if he had a tight bow which he could not draw, he would surely look for a skilful workman.

text_original: For these, the ruler would not employ his relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking, because he knew clearly they were incapable.
text_normalized: For these, the ruler would not employ his relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking, because he knew clearly they were incapable.

text_original: He was afraid they would spoil the things to be attended to.
text_normalized: He was afraid they would spoil the things to be attended to.

text_original: So, in these matters the rulers do not fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.
text_normalized: So, in these matters the rulers do not fail to exalt the virtuous and employ the capable.

text_original: But when it comes to the affairs of the state all is different.
text_normalized: But when it comes to the affairs of the state all is different.

text_original: The relations of the rulers, the rich without merit, and the good-iookino' are all promoted.
text_normalized: The relations of the rulers, the rich without merit, and the good-iookino' are all promoted.

text_original: Then does it not seem that the rulers love their states not even as much as they love a tight bow, a sick horse, a garment, or a cow or a sheep?
text_normalized: Then does it not seem that the rulers love their states not even as much as they love a tight bow, a sick horse, a garment, or a cow or a sheep?

text_original: Therefore I know the gentlemen of the world understand only trifles and not things of significance.
text_normalized: Therefore I know the gentlemen of the world understand only trifles and not things of significance.

text_original: This is like trjdng to make messengers of the dumb and 2n.usical directors of tlie deaf.
text_normalized: This is like trjdng to make messengers of the dumb and 2n.usical directors of tlie deaf.

text_original: To the contrary, in governing the empire the sage-kings of old enriched and honoured those who were not necessarily then relatives, the rich without merit, or the good-looking.
text_normalized: To the contrary, in governing the empire the sage-kings of old enriched and honoured those who were not necessarily then relatives, the rich without merit, or the good-looking.

text_original: At one time Shun cultivated land at Mt.
text_normalized: At one time Shun cultivated land at Mt.

text_original: Li,i made pottery by ; govemmerit of tbe empire and the rule over the people.

text_original: Now, when Yao exalted Shun, T'ang exalted Yi Yin, and Wu Ting exalted Fu Yiieh was it because they were their relatives, the rich without merit, or the good-looking ?
text_normalized: Now, when Yao exalted Shun, T'ang exalted Yi Yin, and Wu Ting exalted Fu Yiieh was it because they were their relatives, the rich without merit, or the good-looking ?

text_original: It was only because that by adopting their views, carrying out their plans, and following their ways, Heaven on high would be blessed, the spirits in the middle would be blessed, and the people below would be blessed.
text_normalized: It was only because that by adopting their views, carrying out their plans, and following their ways, Heaven on high would be blessed, the spirits in the middle would be blessed, and the people below would be blessed.

text_original: Therefore they were promoted and exalted.
text_normalized: Therefore they were promoted and exalted.

text_original: Having understood the principle of exalting the virtuous in government, the ancient sage-kings inscribed it on bamboos and silk  and engraved it on the dishes and vases,  to hand it down to their descendants.
text_normalized: Having understood the principle of exalting the virtuous in government, the ancient sage-kings inscribed it on bamboos and silk  and engraved it on the dishes and vases,  to hand it down to their descendants.

text_original: Thus we find in the Penal Code of Lli a book of an ancient king,- the following : The King said : ‘ Ho !
text_normalized: Thus we find in the Penal Code of Lli a book of an ancient king,- the following : The King said : ‘ Ho !

text_original: come, ye rulers of states and territories, I will tell you how to make punishments a blessing.
text_normalized: come, ye rulers of states and territories, I will tell you how to make punishments a blessing.

text_original: Now it is yours to give repose to the people : — what should you be most concerned about the choosing of ?
text_normalized: Now it is yours to give repose to the people : — what should you be most concerned about the choosing of ?

text_original: Should it not be proper men ?
text_normalized: Should it not be proper men ?

text_original: What should you deal with the most reverently ?
text_normalized: What should you deal with the most reverently ?

text_original: Should it not be punishments ?
text_normalized: Should it not be punishments ?

text_original: What should you calculate the most ?
text_normalized: What should you calculate the most ?

text_original: Should it not be to whom they should reach V  (This is to say) with insight in choosing men and considerate-ness in meting punishments, you can catch up with the ways p.
text_normalized: Should it not be to whom they should reach V  (This is to say) with insight in choosing men and considerate-ness in meting punishments, you can catch up with the ways p.

text_original: By exalta-tion of the virtuous.
text_normalized: By exalta-tion of the virtuous.

text_original: Agaiainthebook “ Shu Nien”,i another boob of an ancient Mng, we find : “ He looked for wise men to protect and aid you.”  This is to say, when the ancient kings reigned over the empire they invariably selected the virtuous and made them officials and aids.
text_normalized: Agaiainthebook “ Shu Nien”,i another boob of an ancient Mng, we find : “ He looked for wise men to protect and aid you.”  This is to say, when the ancient kings reigned over the empire they invariably selected the virtuous and made them officials and aids.

text_original: The gentlemen in the world like riches and honour, and dislike poverty and hmnility.
text_normalized: The gentlemen in the world like riches and honour, and dislike poverty and hmnility.

text_original: Now how can you obtain the former and avoid the latter ?
text_normalized: Now how can you obtain the former and avoid the latter ?

text_original: There is no better way than to practise virtue.
text_normalized: There is no better way than to practise virtue.

text_original: What then is the way to practise virtue ?
text_normalized: What then is the way to practise virtue ?

text_original: Let him who has strength be alert to help others, let him who has wealth endeavour to share it with others, let him who possesses the Tao (the way of nature and life) teach others persuasively.
text_normalized: Let him who has strength be alert to help others, let him who has wealth endeavour to share it with others, let him who possesses the Tao (the way of nature and life) teach others persuasively.

text_original: With this, the hungry will be fed, the cold will be clothed, the disturbed will have order.
text_normalized: With this, the hungry will be fed, the cold will be clothed, the disturbed will have order.

text_original: When the hungry are fed, the cold are clothed, and the disturbed have order— this is procuring abimdant hfe.
text_normalized: When the hungry are fed, the cold are clothed, and the disturbed have order— this is procuring abimdant hfe.

text_original: But those whom the rulers now are enriching and honouring are all eir relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking.
text_normalized: But those whom the rulers now are enriching and honouring are all eir relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking.

text_original: What can there be that guarantees these to be wise ?
text_normalized: What can there be that guarantees these to be wise ?

text_original: When the unwise are charged with the government of the country, disorder in the country can be predicted.
text_normalized: When the unwise are charged with the government of the country, disorder in the country can be predicted.

text_original: Now the gentlemen of the world like riches and honour, and dislike poverty and humihty.
text_normalized: Now the gentlemen of the world like riches and honour, and dislike poverty and humihty.

text_original: But how can you obtain the former and avoid the latter ?
text_normalized: But how can you obtain the former and avoid the latter ?

text_original: There seems to be no EXALTATION OF THE VIETUOUS (III) 53 other way than, to be the rulers’ relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking.
text_normalized: There seems to be no EXALTATION OF THE VIETUOUS (III) 53 other way than, to be the rulers’ relatives, the rich without merit, and the good-looking.

text_original: Evidently one cannot become these by learning.
text_normalized: Evidently one cannot become these by learning.

text_original: So, when the art of judging is not understood, although some virtuous people may even compare with Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu, there will be no commendation.
text_normalized: So, when the art of judging is not understood, although some virtuous people may even compare with Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu, there will be no commendation.

text_original: And, although a relative of the ruler may be lame and dumb, deaf and blind, and evil like Chieh and Chow, there will be no condemnation.
text_normalized: And, although a relative of the ruler may be lame and dumb, deaf and blind, and evil like Chieh and Chow, there will be no condemnation.

text_original: Therefore reward does not fall on the virtuous or punishment on the evil.
text_normalized: Therefore reward does not fall on the virtuous or punishment on the evil.

text_original: As those rewarded are without merit, those punished are naturally without guilt.
text_normalized: As those rewarded are without merit, those punished are naturally without guilt.

text_original: And so, people all became disintegrated in heart and dissipated in body, and despairing in doing good.
text_normalized: And so, people all became disintegrated in heart and dissipated in body, and despairing in doing good.

text_original: With all their strength unused, they would not help one another ; with all unused supplies rotting and fermenting, they would not share with one another; hiding the excellent Tao they would not show it to others.
text_normalized: With all their strength unused, they would not help one another ; with all unused supplies rotting and fermenting, they would not share with one another; hiding the excellent Tao they would not show it to others.

text_original: As a result of this, the hungry are not fed, the cold are not clothed, and the disturbed are not given order.
text_normalized: As a result of this, the hungry are not fed, the cold are not clothed, and the disturbed are not given order.

text_original: In the days of old, Yao had Shun, Shun had Yii, Yii had Kao T’ao, T’ang had Yi Yin, Bang Wu had Hung Yao, T’ai Tien, Nan Kung K’uo and San Yi Sheng — therefore the world was harmonious and people were prosperous.
text_normalized: In the days of old, Yao had Shun, Shun had Yii, Yii had Kao T’ao, T’ang had Yi Yin, Bang Wu had Hung Yao, T’ai Tien, Nan Kung K’uo and San Yi Sheng — therefore the world was harmonious and people were prosperous.

text_original: And those near felt contented and those distant were attracted.
text_normalized: And those near felt contented and those distant were attracted.

text_original: Wherever the sun and the moon shone, boats and vehicles could reach, rain and dew visited, and life depended on grains; few were not converted (to good) by this.
text_normalized: Wherever the sun and the moon shone, boats and vehicles could reach, rain and dew visited, and life depended on grains; few were not converted (to good) by this.

text_original: Hence if the rulers in the world now desire to do magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, and desire to strike tbe way of the sage-kings on the one band and work for tbe benefit of the country and tbe people on tbe other ; then it is indispensable that tbe principle of Exaltation of tbe Virtuous be understood.
text_normalized: Hence if the rulers in the world now desire to do magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, and desire to strike tbe way of the sage-kings on the one band and work for tbe benefit of the country and tbe people on tbe other ; then it is indispensable that tbe principle of Exaltation of tbe Virtuous be understood.

text_original: Now, exaltation of tbe virtuous is indeed tbe blessing of Heaven, tbe spirits, and tbe people, as well as tbe foundation of government.
text_normalized: Now, exaltation of tbe virtuous is indeed tbe blessing of Heaven, tbe spirits, and tbe people, as well as tbe foundation of government.
